explain: Immutable infrastructure replaces servers rather than patching them in-place: create a new AMI with the security patch applied, then replace running instances via Auto Scaling. This eliminates configuration drift, ensures every instance starts from a known-good state, and removes persistence that attackers rely on.

q: Which AWS service provides automated compliance checking against standards like CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark?
options: AWS Config rules evaluating specific resource configurations
AWS Security Hub running automated compliance checks against CIS, PCI DSS, and other standards
Amazon Inspector scanning for known CVE vulnerabilities
AWS Trusted Advisor checking basic security best practices
explain: AWS Security Hub runs automated, continuous compliance checks against industry standards: CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark, PCI DSS, AWS Foundational Security Best Practices, and NIST 800-53. It provides pass/fail findings with remediation guidance, unlike Trusted Advisor which provides general best practice recommendations.

q: A company wants to monitor their EC2 instances for security events and malware. Which AWS service helps?
options: Amazon GuardDuty for threat detection; AWS Inspector for vulnerability scanning
No security monitoring
Security Groups only
CloudWatch metrics only
explain: Amazon GuardDuty detects active threats and anomalous behavior using ML (compromised credentials, unusual API patterns, cryptocurrency mining). Amazon Inspector scans EC2 instances and container images for software vulnerabilities (CVEs) and network exposure. Both are complementary — GuardDuty for threats, Inspector for vulnerabilities.

q: What is a bastion host (jump box) and what modern AWS service replaces the need for one?
options: A dedicated hardware server for cryptographic key operations
An internet-accessible instance used as a gateway to access private instances — AWS Systems Manager Session Manager eliminates the need for one
A monitoring server collecting logs from all EC2 instances in a VPC
A highly available EC2 instance used as a primary application server
explain: Bastion hosts (jump boxes) are EC2 instances in public subnets used as SSH/RDP gateways to private instances — creating a security risk with an internet-accessible entry point. AWS Systems Manager Session Manager provides browser-based shell access to private instances via IAM without any open ports or public IP addresses.

q: Which AWS service provides real-time visibility into your AWS account's adherence to security best practices and compliance standards?
options: Amazon CloudWatch monitoring operational metrics and performance
AWS Security Hub providing continuous security and compliance posture management
Amazon GuardDuty detecting active threats in your account
AWS CloudTrail logging API calls for audit and investigation
explain: AWS Security Hub provides a comprehensive security posture view: aggregates findings from multiple services and partner tools, runs automated compliance checks against multiple standards (CIS, PCI, NIST), displays a security score, and shows prioritized findings with remediation guidance across all linked accounts.

q: A company stores sensitive data in S3 and wants to ensure buckets cannot be made public by accident. Which S3 feature prevents public access?
options: Buckets cannot be protected from public access
S3 encryption only
S3 Block Public Access settings — prevents public ACLs and policies at account and bucket level
S3 bucket policies only
explain: S3 Block Public Access settings prevent public ACLs and public bucket policies from making objects accessible to unauthenticated users — even if developers accidentally add public permissions. AWS recommends enabling S3 Block Public Access at the account level as a baseline security control for all accounts.

q: A multi-account organization wants centralized security policy management and WAF rules across AWS accounts. Which AWS service provides this?
options: Individual account WAF only
AWS Firewall Manager — manages WAF, Shield, and security groups centrally across accounts
CloudFront for CDN rules only
No centralized management
explain: AWS Firewall Manager centrally deploys and manages WAF rules, Shield Advanced protections, Security Groups, and Network Firewall policies across all accounts in AWS Organizations. Changes made in Firewall Manager automatically propagate to member accounts, ensuring consistent security posture without per-account configuration.

q: What is a 'confused deputy' attack and how does the aws:SourceAccount condition key prevent it?
options: When a service acting on your behalf is tricked into acting for another account — aws:SourceAccount ensures the service only acts when called from your account
A social engineering attack targeting AWS account managers
An attack where a confused attacker gets IAM credentials from the wrong account
A man-in-the-middle attack on AWS API calls between services
explain: Confused deputy attacks occur when an AWS service (like CloudTrail, Config) acting on your behalf is tricked by a malicious actor from a different account. The aws:SourceAccount and aws:SourceArn condition keys in resource-based policies ensure the trusted service only performs actions when called from your specific account.

q: A company deploys AWS WAF with rate-based rules. What attack does this protect against?
options: Physical attacks on data centers
Encryption issues
DDoS attacks and brute force attacks — rate-based rules block IPs making excessive requests
No protection from rate-based rules
explain: AWS WAF rate-based rules track the number of requests from each source IP address over a 5-minute window. IPs exceeding the configured threshold (e.g., 1,000 requests per 5 minutes) are automatically blocked, protecting against HTTP flood DDoS attacks and brute force login attempts.

q: A company implements 'zero trust security' in AWS when deploying sensitive applications. What does this security model mean?
options: Trust all users and services by default
Never trust any user or service by default; verify every access request with authentication and least privilege authorization
Only trust AWS services
No authentication needed
explain: Zero trust security assumes no user, service, or network location is inherently trustworthy — every access request must be verified through strong authentication (MFA, certificate-based), authorized via least-privilege policies (IAM), and validated at the resource level regardless of network location.

q: What are EC2 Auto Scaling policies and what is the difference between target tracking and step scaling?
options: They are identical policies with different names for different EC2 families
Auto Scaling policies are for Reserved Instances only; On-Demand uses manual scaling
Target tracking scales horizontally; step scaling scales vertically
Target tracking maintains a metric at a target value (e.g., 50% CPU); step scaling adds/removes capacity in steps based on alarm breach size
explain: Target tracking Auto Scaling policies maintain a specified metric at a target value (e.g., keep CPU utilization at 50%) — AWS automatically adjusts instance count to achieve the target. Step scaling policies add or remove specific numbers of instances based on alarm severity thresholds (add 2 at 70% CPU, add 4 at 85% CPU).

q: A company needs persistent block storage for EC2 instances with high performance. Which AWS service provides this?
options: CloudFront for storage
Amazon EBS (Elastic Block Store) — persistent block storage with multiple volume types (SSD, HDD, provisioned IOPS)
No block storage available
S3 for block storage
explain: Amazon EBS provides persistent block storage volumes that survive EC2 instance stops and terminations. Volume types: gp3/gp2 (general purpose SSD, balanced performance), io2 Block Express (highest IOPS for databases), st1 (throughput-optimized HDD for sequential workloads), and sc1 (cold HDD for archival).

q: A development team wants to automate building, testing, and deploying applications. What AWS service orchestrates this CI/CD pipeline?
options: AWS CodePipeline — automates build, test, and deployment stages with CodeBuild, CodeDeploy integration
CloudFormation for infrastructure only
No CI/CD automation available
S3 for storage only
explain: AWS CodePipeline orchestrates complete CI/CD workflows: source stage (CodeCommit, GitHub, S3), build stage (CodeBuild — compiles code, runs tests), deploy stage (CodeDeploy, ECS, Lambda, Elastic Beanstalk). All stages are automated and triggered automatically on code commits.

q: A company analyzes customer feedback text to understand sentiment. Which AWS service performs natural language analysis?
options: S3 for text storage only
Amazon Comprehend — uses ML to analyze text for sentiment, entities, and topics
EC2 for custom analysis
No text analysis available
explain: Amazon Comprehend uses ML to analyze unstructured text: detecting sentiment (positive/negative/neutral/mixed), extracting key phrases and named entities (people, places, organizations), identifying dominant language, and classifying document topics — all without ML expertise, using pre-trained models.

q: A company wants to build a chatbot for customer service using conversational AI. Which AWS service provides this capability?
options: RDS for chatbot databases only
CloudFront for chatbot delivery only
Amazon Lex — ML service for building conversational chatbots with natural language understanding
No chatbot service available
explain: Amazon Lex provides natural language understanding (NLU) and automatic speech recognition (ASR) to build conversational chatbots and voice interfaces. Lex powers Amazon Alexa — the same technology is available via API for building customer service bots that understand intent and manage multi-turn conversations.

q: A global company operates in multiple languages and wants to automatically translate customer content. Which AWS service provides this?
options: CloudFront for content distribution only
No translation service available
RDS for language storage only
Amazon Translate — translates text between languages using neural machine translation
explain: Amazon Translate uses neural machine translation to translate text between 75+ language pairs with high accuracy for e-commerce product descriptions, customer support interactions, and multilingual content localization — integrating with Comprehend for language detection and Polly for text-to-speech output.

q: A team debugs a microservices application with performance issues across services. Which AWS service visualizes request flows and latency?
options: CloudTrail for API logging only
AWS X-Ray — traces requests through distributed systems, visualizing latency and service dependencies
No tracing service available
CloudWatch metrics only
explain: AWS X-Ray instruments distributed applications by tracing requests from entry point through all downstream service calls (Lambda, DynamoDB, HTTP APIs). The service map visualization shows latency at each hop and identifies which specific service is causing performance bottlenecks in microservices architectures.

q: A company is evaluating AWS and wants to understand the Shared Responsibility Model. Which of the following is AWS responsible for under this model?
options: Configuring security groups and network access control lists
Patching the guest operating system on Amazon EC2 instances
Maintaining the physical security of data center facilities
Managing customer data stored in Amazon S3
explain: Under the AWS Shared Responsibility Model, AWS is responsible for 'security OF the cloud': maintaining physical security of data center facilities, hardware, networking infrastructure, and the hypervisor. Customers are responsible for 'security IN the cloud': OS patching, application security, IAM, and data protection.

q: A business wants to estimate the cost of running an AWS workload before migrating from on-premises. Which AWS tool is designed specifically for this purpose?
options: AWS Trusted Advisor
AWS Budgets
AWS Pricing Calculator
AWS Cost Explorer
explain: The AWS Pricing Calculator (calculator.aws.amazon.com) is designed specifically for pre-deployment cost estimation: select services, configure parameters (instance types, hours per month, data transfer volumes), and it calculates expected monthly costs. Cost Explorer analyzes existing spend; Budgets sets alerts; Pricing Calculator plans future costs.

q: Which AWS service allows a company to consolidate billing across multiple AWS accounts and receive volume pricing discounts by aggregating usage?
options: AWS Budgets
AWS Control Tower
AWS Cost Explorer
AWS Organizations with Consolidated Billing
explain: AWS Organizations with Consolidated Billing combines usage from all linked accounts into a single invoice and aggregates usage for volume pricing tiers. For example, if individual accounts each use 1TB of S3, consolidated billing combines them to potentially qualify for lower per-GB pricing tiers not achievable individually.
